summary:
The sella turcica is positioned below the pituitary gland but above the sphenoid sinus; thus it sits atop or very near this latter structure within the skull base region. \boxed{Sphenoidal Sinus}

The summary concisely captures the critical relationship between the sella turcica’s location relative to other cranial landmarks while adhering strictly to two sentence constraints as requested by your instructions. It also focuses precisely on what was asked about—what body part(s) lie above the sella turcica—and does not include extraneous information beyond those requirements for clarity of response regarding medical knowledge pertinent here.
